title: Readability: canonicalize_display_name doc comment lacks security rationale
description: The doc comment (lines 46–52) lists the steps but omits *why*: NFC normalization combined with bidi control rejection mitigates Unicode spoofing attacks (homoglyph + visual reordering). The distinction between 'reject with error' (bidi controls) vs. 'strip silently' (ZWS) implies intentionality vs. accidental invisible characters, but this intent is not explained. Adding a note like 'Prevents Unicode-based spoofing attacks: canonical homglyphs + RTL override abuse' would improve maintainability.
close_reason: Updated doc comment with Unicode spoofing security rationale; renamed intermediate variables normalized/no_zwc/trimmed for clarity
